text:
There once were a curious squirrel and a wise old owl.

The squirrel had climbed to the highest branch he'd ever been on. His cheeks were full of acorns. He squawked to the owl. "Old wisdom?"

"Hmmm," the owl replied with a quiet hoot. "You've forgotten nothing?"

The squirrel puffed up his little chest proudly. "Nothing at all! I remember every leaf I've ever seen!"

The owl pondered for a moment. "Well," he began pensively, "a leaf from last year here... doesn't look so different."

"Come on!" protested the squirrel. "That leaf has all sorts of memories in tiny pockets!"

"No," the owl said gently. "It's just a leaf."

"The owl knows me!" declared the squirrel excitedly.

"You must be."

* * *

Years went by, and the squirrel grew old. He'd remembered everything. He'd visit that leaf often. One autumn afternoon, he snuck there
